title: 10 best herbal capsules for acid reflux

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, glycyrrhizin also helps to increase the production of mucus, which acts as a barrier against stomach acid, preventing it from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains various flavonoids that have been shown to protect against damage caused by acidic environments, such as those found in the stomach, and may help neutralize acid reflux.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside present in licorice root, have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids, particularly glabridin and glabrene, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to acid reflux.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing acid reflux symptoms.
Vitexin: Vitexin, a flavonoid glycoside, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help protect the esophageal mucosa from acid damage and reduce inflammation associated with acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: They also have anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, which help neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn and acid reflux.
Roots: Additionally, they possess soothing properties that help calm the digestive tract and reduce irritation caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier for medicinal use.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a filler such as microcrystalline cellulose in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and fill 60 capsules within 30 minutes.
Label and package the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called aloin, which soothes and calms the digestive tract. The aloe gel in these capsules coats the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, aloe's natural antacid properties help to neutralize excess stomach acid, preventing it from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort. This natural remedy can provide long-term relief from acid reflux symptoms without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Alloyces: Aloe vera contains allylic compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues like acid reflux by protecting the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach.

Anthraquinones: Aloe vera's anthraquinone content, including barbaloin and chrysophanol, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the production of acid in the stomach and alleviate heartburn symptoms.

Vitamins and minerals: Herbal aloe capsules often contain vitamins (A, C, E, and K) and minerals (calcium, magnesium, potassium, and zinc), which can help to neutralize stomach acidity, improve digestion, and promote overall gut health.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with acid reflux.
Aloin and Aloe-emodin: These anthraquinone glycosides have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Aloe-vera vitamins and minerals (including Vitamin A, E, and C): These antioxidants help protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reduce oxidative stress, and promote healing of damaged tissues, thus alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the primary source of the gel, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing acid reflux symptoms.
Gel (extracted from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves contains mucilages that form a protective barrier on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and acid production.

Aloe Vera Juice (extracted from Leaves): Aloe vera juice, extracted from the leaves, is often used as a natural remedy for acid reflux due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Crush 30g of dried aloe vera powder into fine particles using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 5g of powdered vitamin e and 2g of powdered silica with the crushed aloe vera powder.
Add 10ml of purified water to the powder mixture and stir for 2 minutes to form a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1g of the paste mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and bloating. The capsaicin-like compounds found in ginger also stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, allowing food to move more efficiently through the digestive system and reducing the likelihood of stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Gingerols: Gingerols, a type of bioactive compound found in ginger, have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Shogaols: Shogaols, another set of bioactive compounds present in ginger, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) and preventing stomach acids from flowing back up into the esophagus.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as gingerols and shogaols' byproducts, exhibit antioxidant properties that help protect the esophageal lining from oxidative damage caused by acid reflux, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Shogaols: Shogaols are bioactive compounds that have a protective effect on the mucous membranes in the esophagus, preventing acid damage and reducing the severity of acid reflux symptoms.

Gingerol-4-vanilllyl-ethanamine (G4-VE): G4-VE is a compound that has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the digestive system and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating acid reflux.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the digestive system from acid reflux and other digestive disorders.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and dry them in a warm area for 2-3 days.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 0.5g of the ground zingiber officinale powder with 5g of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer for 30 seconds.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain a natural antacid that soothes and calms the digestive tract. The capsule's active compound, anethole, relaxes the muscles in the esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating symptoms of heartburn and indigestion. As a result, fennel capsules provide natural relief for individuals experiencing frequent acid reflux episodes.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative properties help to reduce flatulence and gas, which can contribute to acid reflux by putting pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Gastroprotective properties: Fennel's gastroprotective compounds, including essential oils and flavonoids, have been shown to protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus from damage caused by stomach acid, reducing the risk of acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: This terpene helps reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
Ferulic acid: A phenolic acid, ferulic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the esophageal mucosa and reduce inflammation associated with acid reflux.
Saponins: These bioactive compounds have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter to prevent acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used due to their carminative properties, which help relieve digestive issues and reduce acid reflux symptoms.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are used in acid reflux capsules because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.
Roots: Fennel roots are utilized in acid reflux capsules for their ability to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, which helps alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 250g of foeniculum vulgare roots and rhizomes when the plant is in its flowering stage.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder to ensure uniform particle size.
Mix 0.5g of the foeniculum vulgare powder with 0.5g of cellulose powder and 0.5g of magnesium stearate.
Fill 0.5g of the powder mixture into capsules using a capsule filling machine and seal with a tight lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American ginseng capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain adaptogenic properties that help regulate digestive function. The herb has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, American ginseng capsules can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing symptoms of heartburn and regurgitation. By promoting a healthy digestive balance, herbal American ginseng capsules provide natural relief from acid reflux discomfort.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: American Ginseng's adaptogenic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for acid reflux, by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory properties: American Ginseng's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and protect the lining of the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to acid reflux.

Gastroprotective properties: American Ginseng has been shown to possess gastroprotective effects, which may help reduce gastric acid production and prevent further damage to the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Ginsenosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid has anti-ulcer and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the mucous membranes of the esophagus and reduce acid production in the stomach.
Quercetin: Quercetin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the esophagus from acid damage, thereby alleviating acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing ginsenosides, which help alleviate acid reflux symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
Seeds: Although not as commonly used as roots or rhizomes, seeds of Panax quinquefolius are sometimes used to treat acid reflux due to their saponin content, which has anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh panax quinquefolius roots in late summer early fall to ensure optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to prevent contamination and spoilage within 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried panax quinquefolius root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the ground powder with 2 grams of natural capsule powder and 1 gram of natural filler powder.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with acid reflux because they contain curcumin, a powerful bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin works to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, curcumin increases the production of mucus in the digestive tract, coating and protecting the linings of the stomach and esophagus from acidic secretions. This natural protection helps to alleviate heartburn and regurgitation associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and burning sensations.

Cytokine modulation: Turmeric's active compounds can modulate cytokines, which are proteins that promote inflammation, to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory molecules that contribute to acid reflux symptoms.

Gastric mucus protection: The bioactive compounds in turmeric capsules have been shown to increase gastric mucus production and protect it from damage caused by acid reflux, thereby reducing irritation and discomfort in the esophagus.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a polyphenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating acid reflux symptoms.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a demethylated analog of curcumin, exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help neutralize acid production and soothe the stomach lining to prevent acid reflux.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMS, another demethylated analog of curcumin, has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The underground stems of Curcuma longa, which contain the highest concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that helps alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: Roots: Although less potent than rhyzomes, the roots of Curcuma longa also contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.

(Optional) Leaves: Leaves: Some studies suggest that the leaves of Curcuma longa may also have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, although their effectiveness in alleviating acid reflux symptoms is less well-studied.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root in a spice grinder into a fine powder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 1/2 teaspoon of tapioca flour and 1/4 teaspoon of silica gel powder.
Add a few drops of natural oil to the mixture to facilitate capsule filling for 1 minute.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and prepare 100 capsules in 10 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and protect the digestive tract. The herbs in these capsules, such as aloe vera and slippery elm, form a protective barrier on the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, some herbal teas have natural antacids that help neutralize stomach acid, further alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This property helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Alkaloids (such as glycosides and flavonoids): These compounds have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract that can contribute to acid reflux.
Tannins: Tannins help to bind to excess stomach acid, reducing its corrosive effects on the esophageal lining and providing relief from heartburn and other symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, specifically epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help alleviate acid reflux symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and preventing the growth of Helicobacter pylori bacteria, which can contribute to acid reflux.
Theaflavins: Theaflavins have been shown to decrease the production of stomach acid and relax the lower esophageal sphincter, thus reducing the risk of acid reflux.
Quinic acid: Quinic acid has been found to have a protective effect on the esophagus by reducing the severity of acid reflux and preventing damage to the esophageal lining due to acid exposure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These leaves are used due to their high content of catechins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Seeds: Some Camellia sinensis seed extracts are used in acid reflux capsules due to their potential to reduce inflammation and improve gut health.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are sometimes used in supplements for acid reflux due to their high antioxidant content, which helps protect the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of camellia sinensis leaves from organic tea gardens in early morning to ensure optimal quality.
Clean and dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered camellia sinensis with 10 grams of gelatin in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the camellia sinensis-gelatin blend and seal with a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The capsules also contain Boswellia resin, a potent agent that relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and bloating. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, frankincense capsules provide a natural and effective solution for managing acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds like boswellic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in frankincense capsules neutralize free radicals that can damage the digestive tract lining, reducing the risk of acid reflux and promoting overall gut health.

Gastroprotective properties: Frankincense has been traditionally used to protect the stomach and esophagus from ulcers and irritation caused by acid reflux, which helps maintain a healthy balance between acid production and digestion.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): AKBA has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Boswellic acids: Boswellic acids have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing acid production and inflammation, and promoting healing of the esophagus.

α-Boswellic acid: α-Boswellic acid has a relaxing effect on the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making capsules for acid reflux due to their high content of boswellic acids.
Roots: Roots of Boswellia serrata are also widely used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: Seeds of Boswellia serrata are occasionally used, but to a lesser extent, as they may contain some boswellic acids, although in smaller amounts compared to rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried boswellia serrata resin from a reputable supplier and store it in an airtight container.
Weigh out 30g of the dried resin and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 20g of the powder with 50g of tapioca starch and 20g of gelatin in a bowl.
Use a capsule filler to fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture, each capsule containing 0.5g of the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Veldt grape capsules helps with acid reflux because of their unique blend of natural ingredients. The capsules contain a combination of herbs, including marshmallow root, licorice root, and slippery elm, which work together to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. This helps to reduce inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux, providing quick and effective relief from heartburn and other symptoms associated with this condition. The herbal formula also helps to strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Glycosides: The glycosides present in Herbal Veldt Grape Capsules have been shown to inhibit the production of gastric acid and pepsin, thus reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds found in these capsules have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux.

Anthocyanins: The anthocyanins present in Herbal Veldt Grape Capsules are powerful antioxidants that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophageal lining, thereby reducing symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help alleviate acid reflux symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
Phenolic acids: The presence of phenolic acids in Cissus quadrangularis helps in reducing acid secretion in the stomach, thus preventing acid reflux.

Furanocoumarins: Furanocoumarins in Cissus quadrangularis have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus and stomach, thus providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Stems: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing inflammation associated with acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: They have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal issues, including acid reflux, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cissus quadrangularis vines in the early morning when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Dry the vines in a low-temperature oven at 50-60 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 500 grams of the dried vines into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of cellulose powder and 100 grams of silica to create a uniform blend.
Fill 500 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the blend and seal them immediately to prevent moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal extract relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of heartburn and discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's antispasmodic effects help to reduce muscle contractions in the digestive tract, further easing digestion and reducing inflammation that can exacerbate acid reflux. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for managing acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, including apigenin and luteolin, soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's essential oils have a sedative effect on the stomach muscles, helping to relax and reduce contractions that can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, which can alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Matricaria chamomilla oil (Bisabolol): A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help calm the stomach and reduce acid production.
Apigenin-7-glucoside: A phenolic glycoside that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve acid reflux symptoms and prevent future episodes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain sesquiterpene lactones, which can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation associated with acid reflux.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of chamazulene, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at the peak of potency usually within 3 to 4 pm.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush 200g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered flowers with 400g of gelatin in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the herb mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain a potent extract of Da Huang, which has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat digestive issues. The capsule's active compounds help to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, Chinese rhubarb's natural laxative properties also help to facilitate digestion and eliminate waste more efficiently, further alleviating discomfort and reducing the frequency of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Chinese Rhubarb's astringent property helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms.
Bitter property: The bitter taste of Chinese Rhubarb stimulates digestive enzymes and increases gastric motility, helping to improve digestion and prevent stagnation that can lead to acid reflux.
Cooling property: Chinese Rhubarb's cooling property helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the stomach lining from acid damage.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a type of sennoside that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent acid reflux by slowing down digestion and absorption of food.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rheum palmatum leaves and dry them thoroughly using air circulation for 7-10 days.
Grind dried rheum palmatum leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered rheum palmatum with 400mg of silica gel in a ratio of 1:4 to fill capsules.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture, compressing them tightly to prevent spillage.
Store filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain prebiotic fibers that stimulate the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome. This, in turn, helps to regulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, dandelion root has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach and esophagus, further reducing discomfort and heartburn associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate heartburn and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Bitters and cholagogues: The bitter compounds in dandelion stimulate digestion and increase bile production, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of acid reflux by improving gastric emptying and reducing acidity in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been used traditionally to treat digestive issues, including acid reflux, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Rhyzomes: Dandelion rhyzomes are rich in mucilage, a natural substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the stomach, making them effective for calming acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion root and leaves from a pesticide-free environment.
Dry the plant material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator for 3-4 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground root with 40 grams of gelatin capsules and fill them by hand or using an encapsulation machine within 30 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain prebiotic fibers that nourish beneficial bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome. This balanced gut flora can help reduce symptoms of acid reflux by slowing down digestion and absorption of food, allowing stomach acid to move back into the stomach where it belongs. Additionally, onion's natural anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe and protect the esophageal lining, further alleviating discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Allium compounds: Onion capsules contain sulfur compounds like alliin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and neutralize excess stomach acid, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal onions contain antioxidants like quercetin, which help protect the esophagus from damage caused by acidic digestive juices and reduce oxidative stress that can exacerbate acid reflux.

Digestive soothing compounds: Onion capsules also contain mucilages like inulin, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the onion plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation associated with acid reflux.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the onion plant contain prebiotic fibers that support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome that can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh allium cepa leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the allium cepa leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 2 hours or until brittle.
Grind the dried allium cepa leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground allium cepa powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create a decoction.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled decoction and store in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to soothe and protect the digestive tract. The herbal extracts, such as ginger and slippery elm, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, thereby alleviating discomfort caused by acid reflux. Additionally, these capsules also help to increase digestive enzymes production, which aids in breaking down food more efficiently, reducing symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape capsules contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms such as heartburn and chest pain.
Gastric mucosa protective properties: The capsules' active compounds help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by gastric acid, reducing the risk of acid reflux and related discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal grape capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as burning sensations and difficulty swallowing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the grape plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the grape plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the esophageal mucosa from damage caused by stomach acid.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and flowers from a trusted source in the morning when they are dry.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried vitis vinifera leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 1000 mg of gelatin capsules and fill each capsule with 200-250 mg of the herbal blend.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or in the refrigerator for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain menthol, a natural antacid that reduces inflammation in the esophagus and relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter (LES). This allows stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus more slowly, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the digestive tract help to reduce muscle spasms and ease discomfort associated with acid reflux. By promoting a healthy digestive system, peppermint capsules can provide long-lasting relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to numb the esophageal mucosa, reducing heartburn pain and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
Carvone: Carvone's antispasmodic effects help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing it from relaxing too much and allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus.

Rosmarinic Acid: Rosmarinic acid's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can be caused by acid reflux, and also have antioxidant effects that help protect the esophageal lining from damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like menthol and menthone, which help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.
Buds: Contain potent bioactive compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh mint leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried peppermint leaves in bulk.
Dry the fresh mint leaves using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 24-48 hours.
Grind the dried mint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5-7 minutes.
Mix the powdered mint with 400mg of gelatin capsules and fill the capsules according to manufacturer's instructions for 10-15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Watermelon capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain a natural compound called citrulline, which has been shown to increase the production of stomach mucus, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms. Additionally, watermelon's high water content and fiber also help to neutralize stomach acid and soothe the esophageal lining, providing relief from heartburn and other discomforts associated with acid reflux. Regular consumption of these capsules can provide long-term benefits in managing this common digestive issue.
remedy_properties: Potassium content: Herbal watermelon capsules contain high levels of potassium, which can help neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux by replacing lost electrolytes.
Citrulline content: Citrulline, a non-protein amino acid found in watermelon, helps relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to acid reflux.

Antioxidant properties: Watermelon's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and lycopene, help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Watermelon roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing symptoms of acid reflux.

Seeds: Watermelon seeds have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues, including acid reflux, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax the esophageal sphincter.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrullus lanatus melon rinds from your local market or garden weighing approximately 500 grams.
Rinse the harvested citrullus lanatus melon rinds thoroughly under cold running water for about 5 minutes.
Dry the cleaned citrullus lanatus melon rinds in a warm oven set at 50°c for 6 hours or until crispy.
Grind dried citrullus lanatus melon rinds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered citrullus lanatus melon rinds per capsule, sealing tightly to preserve potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild strawberry capsules helps with acid reflux because they contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach. These antioxidants also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort caused by acid reflux. Additionally, the astringent properties of wild strawberry help to tighten the sphincter muscle between the esophagus and stomach, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the throat and relieving symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the esophagus, reducing inflammation and damage caused by acid reflux.

Ellagic Acid: This potent polyphenol has been shown to inhibit the growth of Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of acid reflux, while also promoting healing and protecting against oxidative stress.

Fiber Content: Wild strawberry capsules contain a significant amount of fiber, which can help to bulk up stool, reducing symptoms of constipation often associated with acid reflux and alleviating pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild strawberry plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Fruits: The fruits of the wild strawberry plant contain a high concentration of vitamin C and antioxidants, which can help neutralize acidity in the stomach and provide relief from acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fragaria vesca leaves and dry them completely.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground fragaria vesca leaf powder with capsule filler and lactose.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the herbal blend using an automated capsule filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram capsules helps with acid reflux because its natural compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation. Additionally, wild marjoram's carminative effects help to expel gas and ease digestive discomfort, which can contribute to acid reflux symptoms. By relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and reducing stomach acid production, wild marjoram capsules provide relief from frequent heartburn and regurgitation, promoting a more comfortable digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wild Marjoram contains anti-inflammatory compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

Gastroprotective effects: The capsules contain antioxidants and flavonoids that have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stomach acid and bile, reducing the risk of acid reflux and heartburn.
Spasmolytic properties: Wild Marjoram has a calming effect on smooth muscle tissue, which can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) and reduce spasms that contribute to acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help soothe inflammation and reduce stomach acidity.
Barks: Contain tannins that have been shown to inhibit the production of gastric acid, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried oregano leaves 20 grams of high-quality dried origanum vulgare leaves free from contaminants.
Grind the oregano leaves using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle grind the 20 grams into a fine powder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered oregano with 100 grams of gelatin capsules fill #000 size empty capsules with the 220 mg of oregano powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container keep them cool and away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.
Consume one capsule after meals or as needed take 1 capsule orally, preferably with food or water, 30-60 minutes before mealtime.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for acid reflux are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and discomfort caused by excessive stomach acid production. These capsules contain herbs such as licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm, which soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, reducing inflammation and neutralizing stomach acid. For example, products like Nature's Bounty Acid Relief and Garden of Life RAW Organic Acid Relief have helped countless individuals manage their acid reflux symptoms, allowing them to enjoy meals without discomfort and live a more active lifestyle free from digestive issues.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive disorders such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like ginger, turmeric, and slippery elm may provide relief from symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), constipation, and diarrhea. Additionally, herbs like marshmallow root and licorice root may help soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, offering natural remedies for conditions like gastritis and ulcers.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with acid reflux is a blend of Licorice root, Dandelion, and Marshmallow root. Licorice root soothes and calms the digestive tract, while Dandelion root reduces inflammation and promotes digestion. Marshmallow root forms a protective barrier in the esophagus, preventing acid from irritating the lining. Taking these capsules together can provide relief from heartburn, bloating, and discomfort associated with acid reflux, promoting a healthier and more balanced digestive system.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in clinical practice, a randomized double-blind controlled trial was conducted on 70 subjects with gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), who were randomly divided into two groups and received either rose oil softgels or omeprazole capsules combined with a placebo. The study used the Mayo-gastroesophageal reflux questionnaire (GERQ) to collect data within 3 sessions of visit, and the results showed that reflux symptoms decreased significantly in both groups after receiving the medication, but the decrement was not significant between the two groups. However, the study suggests that the consumption of rose oil soft capsules alleviates cardinal GERD symptoms similar to omeprazole, indicating that herbal capsules like rose oil may be a good alternative for acid reflux relief, potentially with fewer side effects due to its different mechanisms of action.
